attributes.action: Against the replacement of English historical figures on UK bank notes.
attributes.additional_details: Replacing English historical figures on new UK banknotes is a misguided decision that risks erasing important parts of our national heritage and identity. These figures, such as Shakespeare, Churchill, and Darwin, embody the values, achievements, and cultural milestones that have shaped the nation. Removing them diminishes our historical narrative and disrespects the contributions of those who have significantly impacted society.
attributes.background:
i request that we keep our historical ancestors on ur uk bank notes.
Instead of erasing these icons, we should celebrate and educate future generations about their legacy, ensuring that our history remains visible and valued on the currency that circulates through everyday life.

rejection.details:
Bank notes are the responsibility of the Bank of England, which is independent of the Government and Parliament.

You can find out how the Bank decides who will be on bank notes here:
https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/banknotes/banknote-characters
